meta.title: Frank-Frost Family Tree
meta.description: Genealogy database covering the Frost (maternal) and Frank/Lewis/Bickford/Fay (paternal) family lines, Whitney/Mosher lines (Kevin's maternal grandparents). Traces Canadian ancestry through the Fay family of Quebec. Also covers the Miller/Richards/Sacre lines (Pauline's family).

individuals.burial_place: Hill Crest Cemetery, Lagrange, ME (presumed)
individuals.notes: Father of 7 children. Served in Civil War as cook's assistant. Military service began 30 April 1861 at Medford, age 41. Also enlisted 23 December 1863 at Sidney (Kennebec County). Second enlistment in Sidney, Kennebec County (1863) indicates return to his birth county after 1860. A Mark Frost (birth estimated 1819, age 58) appears in Maine Deaths and Burials dying 19 March 1877 in Augusta, Kennebec — consistent with age and county. Death date assigned medium-high confidence. 1870 CENSUS ANOMALY RESOLVED (session 3, 2026-03-31): The Rome, Kennebec Mark Frost with 'Lucy A Frost' (ark:/61903/1:1:M6D2-T6J) is a DIFFERENT person — FamilySearch lists separate Mark Frost persons with spouses 'Lucy A Frost' and 'Matilda Orcutt' in Kennebec County. Catherine Gibberd's FamilySearch profile (K6QM-FM6) records 1870 residence as Augusta, Kennebec (0 sources) — she was likely at the Maine Insane Hospital by this date, consistent with her confirmed 1900 residence there. Our Mark Frost was likely also in the Augusta area near Catherine's institution, and died there 19 March 1877. This resolution elevates death record confidence from 'medium' to 'medium-high'. FamilySearch PID: GQ4X-WZ1.
individuals.source_citations: William H. Frost death certificate; FindAGrave Catherine E. Gibberd Frost memorial #113000115; FamilySearch GQ4X-WZ1; Maine Births and Christenings 1739-1900 (ark:/61903/1:1:F44Z-6JQ); Maine Civil War Soldiers and Sailors Card Index 1861-1865 (ark:/61903/1:1:246N-2XK); Maine Deaths and Burials 1841-1910 — Mark Frost d.19 Mar 1877 Augusta (ark:/61903/1:1:F48P-2M6) [medium confidence]; 1870 US Census — Mark Frost, Rome, Kennebec, ME (ark:/61903/1:1:M6D2-T6J) [uncertain identity]
individuals.interesting_facts:
Mark Frost was born on April 21, 1818, in Belgrade, Kennebec County, Maine, the son of Benjamin Frost. He grew up in central Maine's farming country and married Catherine E. Gibberd on March 11, 1847, in Skowhegan, Somerset County. The couple settled into a life of farming, first in the Kilmarnock area (where they appear in the 1850 census) and later in Medford, Piscataquis County, where they were established by 1860. Together they raised seven children: Sarah, William, James, Clara, John Lyman, Etta Clarista, and Nellie Burton.

When the Civil War broke out, Mark answered the call despite being 41 years old — well above the typical enlistment age. He mustered in on April 30, 1861, at Medford and served as a cook's assistant, supporting the troops from behind the front lines. After his initial service, he enlisted a second time on December 23, 1863, this time in Sidney, Kennebec County, suggesting he had returned to his birth region. His willingness to serve twice speaks to a deep sense of duty during a turbulent era in American history.

Mark's later years remain partially obscured by the passage of time. By 1870, Catherine appears to have been residing in Augusta — possibly already at the Maine Insane Hospital where she would spend decades. A Maine Deaths and Burials record places Mark's death on March 19, 1877, in Augusta, Kennebec County, at approximately age 58. He is believed to be buried at Hill Crest Cemetery in Lagrange, the same cemetery where several of his children and grandchildren would later rest, binding the Frost family to this small Penobscot County community for generations.

Mark's legacy lives on through his descendants. His son John Lyman Frost settled in Bangor and became a direct ancestor of the family. His children scattered across Maine and New England — Clara married into the Hubbard family, Etta became a schoolteacher in Connecticut, and Nellie was adopted and raised in Massachusetts. Despite the hardships of frontier farming and war, Mark and Catherine's family endured and grew across the region.

individuals.burial_place: Hill Crest Cemetery, Lagrange, ME
individuals.notes: Maiden name Gibberd (variant: Gibherd/Gilbert). Father was a carpenter born in Germany. Mother born in France. Death record confirms Skowhegan birth and age 80. Married Mark Frost 11 Mar 1847 in Skowhegan, Somerset, ME. Resided: 1860 Medford, ME; 1870 Augusta, ME; 1880 Augusta, ME; 1900 Maine Insane Hospital, Augusta; 1910 Bangor Ward 7, ME. FamilySearch PID: K6QM-FM6.
individuals.source_citations: FindAGrave #113000115; Catherine's death record per FindAGrave bio; FamilySearch K6QM-FM6
individuals.residence: Medford, ME (1860); Augusta, ME (1870-1900); Bangor, ME (1910)
individuals.interesting_facts:
Catherine E. Gibberd was born around 1830 in Skowhegan, Somerset County, Maine, to an immigrant family — her father was a carpenter born in Germany, and her mother was born in France. The surname Gibberd appeared in various spellings including Gibherd and Gilbert, reflecting the fluid nature of 19th-century record-keeping. Growing up in the mill town of Skowhegan on the Kennebec River, Catherine came of age in a community that was rapidly developing during Maine's early statehood years.

On March 11, 1847, Catherine married Mark Frost in Skowhegan, and the young couple began building their family. They moved to the Kilmarnock area and then to Medford in Piscataquis County, where they were farming by 1860. Catherine bore seven children between roughly 1848 and 1859: Sarah, William, James, Clara, John Lyman, Etta Clarista, and Nellie Burton. Life on a Maine farm was demanding, and the family faced additional hardship when Mark left to serve in the Civil War in 1861.

Catherine's life took a tragic turn at some point after Mark's death in 1877. By the 1880 census she was living in Augusta, and by 1900 she was a patient at the Maine Insane Hospital in Augusta, where she appears in the census that year. The circumstances that led to her institutionalization are lost to history, but she spent many years there. By 1910, at roughly age 80, she had relocated to Bangor, where she was living in Ward 7.

Catherine died on August 23, 1910, in Lagrange, Penobscot County, at the age of 79 or 80. Her death record confirms her Skowhegan birthplace and her age. She was laid to rest at Hill Crest Cemetery in Lagrange alongside her husband Mark, reunited in death in the community where several of their children had settled. Her FindAGrave memorial (#113000115) preserves her memory and provides biographical details about the Frost family.

individuals.burial_place: Hill Crest Cemetery, Lagrange, ME
individuals.notes: Death cert lists Catherine as mother. Wife: Esther M Thompson (m. 18 Mar 1874, Woodland, Aroostook, ME). 5 children: William Leslie, Harriet May, James W, Winfield C., Cora B. Resided: 1850 Kilmarnock, ME; 1860 Medford, ME; 1880 Chapman, Aroostook, ME. FamilySearch PID: GQ4F-WP6.
individuals.source_citations: Death certificate, State of Maine; FindAGrave #113000045; FamilySearch GQ4F-WP6
individuals.residence: Kilmarnock, ME (1850); Medford, ME (1860); Chapman, Aroostook, ME (1880)
individuals.interesting_facts:
William H. Frost was born on May 24, 1849, in Perry, Washington County, Maine — a coastal town near the Canadian border, far from where the family later settled. He was the eldest son of Mark and Catherine (Gibberd) Frost, and grew up on the family farm, first in Kilmarnock (1850 census) and then in Medford, Piscataquis County (1860 census). William was just twelve years old when his father Mark left to serve in the Civil War.

As a young man, William struck out on his own, heading north to Aroostook County — Maine's vast agricultural frontier. On March 18, 1874, he married Esther M. Thompson in Woodland, Aroostook County, and the couple settled in Chapman, where William worked as a farmer. They raised five children together: William Leslie, Harriet May, James W., Winfield C., and Cora B. The 1880 census records the family in Chapman, with William working the land in one of Maine's most productive farming regions.

Tragically, William died young. On November 8, 1894, at just 45 years and 6 months of age, he passed away in Bradford, Maine, from heart disease. His death certificate confirms his mother as Catherine, connecting him definitively to the Mark and Catherine Frost family. He was buried at Hill Crest Cemetery in Lagrange, the family's ancestral burial ground, alongside his parents and siblings.

William's death left Esther a widow with young children to raise. His story reflects the hard realities of 19th-century rural Maine — early death, demanding physical labor, and the constant challenge of wresting a living from the northern soil. Yet his legacy endured through his five children, who carried the Frost name into the 20th century.

individuals.notes: Child of Mark and Catherine Frost. FamilySearch PID: LTMR-ZQ5. Resided: 1850 Kilmarnock, ME; 1860 Medford, Piscataquis, ME (confirmed in 1860 census). SESSION 3 FINDING (2026-03-31): Found in 1865 Massachusetts State Census (ark:/61903/1:1:MQH5-3Q1) in Haverhill, Essex County, MA — age 17, born 1848 Maine, single, living in household of Nathaniel Frost (age 61, born 1804 Maine, widowed). Nathaniel Frost (PID M665-WYH, 1804-1879) is confirmed as Benjamin Frost's 8th child — Mark Frost's older brother and Sarah's uncle. Sarah left Maine as a teenager to live with extended family in Massachusetts, paralleling sister Nellie Burton Frost who also moved to MA young. The 1865 record is linked to her 1860 census entry via FamilySearch 'Similar Records', confirming identity. Other household members: Nathaniel Frost Jr (35), Loring W Frost (27), Helen Frost (20, NH), Lorinda Frost (38), Caroline A Frost (30), Fidelia Frost (28), Ellen L Frost (19). Also noted: NJ marriage record (ark:/61903/1:1:FZGK-VQ1) shows a 'Sarah F. Frost' (age 42, birth ~1847, U.S.) marrying Robert Waldron on 1 Oct 1889 in Rahway, Union, NJ — low confidence match (birth year close but no state-level birthplace confirmation; listed as previously married). ELIMINATED: 1880 Census Hampden, Penobscot 'Sarah F. Frost' (ark:/61903/1:1:MFQM-549) married to 'Chas. Frost' — this woman's maiden name was Sara Freeman Gorton per Maine Vital Records and FindAGrave; she is a different person. No records after 1865 confirmed for our Sarah.
individuals.source_citations: Catherine Gibberd Frost FindAGrave bio; FamilySearch LTMR-ZQ5; 1865 Massachusetts State Census (ark:/61903/1:1:MQH5-3Q1) — Sarah F Frost, age 17, Haverhill, Essex, MA [medium confidence]; 1860 US Census Medford, Piscataquis (ark:/61903/1:1:MDHT-MZ2)
individuals.residence: Kilmarnock, ME (1850); Medford, Piscataquis, ME (1860); Haverhill, Essex, MA (1865)
individuals.interesting_facts:
Sarah F. Frost was born in 1848 in Maine, the eldest child of Mark and Catherine (Gibberd) Frost. She appears in the 1850 census with her family in Kilmarnock and in the 1860 census in Medford, Piscataquis County, growing up on the family farm alongside her siblings.

Sarah's life took an unusual turn as a teenager. By 1865, at just seventeen years old, she had left Maine entirely and was living in Haverhill, Essex County, Massachusetts, in the household of Nathaniel Frost — her father Mark's older brother. The 1865 Massachusetts State Census records her as single, age 17, born in Maine. Nathaniel Frost (1804–1879), Benjamin Frost's eighth child, had settled in Massachusetts and took in his young niece. The household included several other Frost family members: Nathaniel Jr., Loring W., and several women named Frost.

Sarah's move to Massachusetts paralleled that of her younger sister Nellie Burton Frost, who was also sent to live with relatives in the Boston area — a pattern that suggests the family may have been struggling after Mark's departure for the Civil War. Being placed with extended family was common in 19th-century New England when parents faced hardship.

After 1865, Sarah's trail grows cold. Researchers have investigated a possible match with a Sarah F. Frost who married Robert Waldron on October 1, 1889, in Rahway, New Jersey — she was age 42, born approximately 1847, and listed as previously married — but the connection remains unconfirmed. An 1880 census match in Hampden, Penobscot County has been definitively eliminated as a different woman (Sara Freeman Gorton). Sarah's ultimate fate — whether she married, had children, or where she spent her adult years — remains one of the family's unsolved mysteries.

individuals.burial_place: Hill Crest Cemetery, Lagrange, ME

individuals.source_citations: FindAGrave #113000069; FamilySearch GQ4X-RRV
individuals.interesting_facts:
James L. Frost was born on December 29, 1851, a son of Mark and Catherine (Gibberd) Frost. He grew up alongside his siblings in Medford, Piscataquis County, Maine, during a period when the family was farming in Maine's interior. James was about nine years old when his father Mark enlisted for Civil War service in 1861.

James's life remains one of the less documented among the Frost siblings. Unlike his brother William who moved to Aroostook County or his sisters who relocated to Massachusetts and Connecticut, James appears to have remained closer to the family's home territory in the Penobscot County area. His burial at Hill Crest Cemetery in Lagrange — the same cemetery where his parents Mark and Catherine, his brother William, and his brother John Lyman rest — suggests he maintained strong ties to the family's community throughout his life.

James lived to the age of 77, dying on November 23, 1929, making him one of the longer-lived members of his generation in the Frost family. His brother William had died at just 45, and his father Mark at approximately 58. James's longevity meant he witnessed extraordinary changes — from the Civil War era of his childhood through the Roaring Twenties.

His FamilySearch record (PID: GQ4X-RRV) and FindAGrave memorial (#113000069) at Hill Crest Cemetery preserve his place in the family's history, though many details of his occupation, marriage, and daily life await further research in census and local records.

individuals.burial_place: Bradford, Penobscot County, Maine (per FamilySearch)
individuals.notes: Married name Hubbard. Married Philander L Hubbard (ID 148, 1831-1906) ~1870, Fairfield, Somerset, ME. Five children: Cora E Hubbard (1871-1898), Lenora F Hubbard (1873-1930), Wilton Sherman Hubbard (1876-1950), Wilbur Hubbard (1876-Deceased), Ernest Mark Hubbard (1877-Deceased). Child named Mark E Hubbard in 1880 census — named after Clara's father Mark Frost. Family moved: Fairfield Somerset ME → Chapman Aroostook ME (1880) → Bradford/Bangor Penobscot ME (1900-1930). Burial per FamilySearch: Williams Cemetery, Bradford, Penobscot, ME (near husband Philander). FamilySearch PID: GQ4F-7LH (13 sources). SESSION 3 FINDINGS (2026-03-31): 1900 Census (ark:/61903/1:1:MMLR-SV4): Clara R Hubbard, age 47, born Aug 1853, Maine, married 30 years, 5 children total / 3 living, listed as 'Sister' of household head John L Frost (age 45) — her brother John Lyman Frost (ID 7). Father's birthplace: Maine (=Mark Frost), Mother's birthplace: Maine (=Catherine Gibberd). Confirms Clara is daughter of Mark and Catherine Frost. 1930 Census (ark:/61903/1:1:XMD7-PCS): Clara R Hubbard, age 75, widowed, Bradford, Penobscot, ME. Listed as 'mother-in-law' of household head Fred L Jones (55). Son Ernest M Hubbard (52) also in household. Not found in 1940 census — died between April 1930 and April 1940 (age ~77-87). Death date remains unknown. Neither FindAGrave nor Nathan Hale Cemetery Collection record a death date. Residences: 1850 Norway, Oxford, ME; 1860 Medford, Piscataquis, ME; 1880 Chapman, Aroostook, ME; 1900 Bangor Ward 1, Penobscot, ME; 1910-1930 Bradford, Penobscot, ME.
individuals.source_citations: FindAGrave #130371604; FamilySearch GQ4F-7LH (13 sources); FamilySearch Family Tree LC33-JRJ (Philander Hubbard); 1900 US Census (ark:/61903/1:1:MMLR-SV4) — Clara as 'Sister' of John L Frost, Bangor, Penobscot; 1930 US Census (ark:/61903/1:1:XMD7-PCS) — Clara age 75, widowed, Bradford, Penobscot; Maine Nathan Hale Cemetery Collection (ark:/61903/1:1:QVJ5-4VWL) — burial Williams Cemetery, Bradford
individuals.residence: Norway, Oxford, ME (1850); Medford, Piscataquis, ME (1860); Chapman, Aroostook, ME (1880); Bangor, Penobscot, ME (1900); Bradford, Penobscot, ME (1910-1930)
individuals.interesting_facts:
Clara R. Frost was born in August 1853 in Maine, the fourth child and second daughter of Mark and Catherine (Gibberd) Frost. She appears in the 1850 census in Norway, Oxford County, and in the 1860 census in Medford, Piscataquis County, growing up on the family farm.

Around 1870, Clara married Philander L. Hubbard in Fairfield, Somerset County, Maine. Philander was considerably older — born in 1831, he was 22 years Clara's senior. Despite the age difference, the couple built a large family together, welcoming five children: Cora E. (1871–1898), Lenora F. (1873–1930), Wilton Sherman (1876–1950), Wilbur (1876–deceased), and Ernest Mark (1877–deceased). The naming of their youngest son Mark after Clara's father is a touching tribute to the Frost patriarch.

The Hubbard family was mobile, following opportunity across Maine. From Fairfield in Somerset County, they moved to Chapman in Aroostook County (1880 census), where Clara's brother William was also farming. By 1900, Clara was living in Bangor Ward 1, Penobscot County, listed in the census as the "Sister" of household head John L. Frost — her brother John Lyman. The 1900 census records her as age 47, born August 1853, married 30 years, with 5 children born and 3 living, confirming the early deaths of two of her children.

After Philander's death in 1906, Clara continued living in the Penobscot County area. The 1930 census finds her in Bradford, age 75, widowed, living in the household of her son-in-law Fred L. Jones, with son Ernest M. Hubbard also present. Clara is believed to have died between 1930 and 1940, as she does not appear in the 1940 census. She was buried at Williams Cemetery in Bradford, Penobscot County, near her husband Philander.

individuals.burial_place: Hill Crest Cemetery, Lagrange, ME
individuals.notes: Kevin's great-great-grandfather. Full name John Lyman Frost. Married Carrie Wilder Storer. Marriage date: June 2, 1878 per FamilySearch; June 9, 1878 per Piscataquis Observer. Of Orneville at time of marriage. Resided: 1860 Medford; 1880 Orneville; 1900-1920 Bangor. FamilySearch PID: M6LP-K52.

individuals.residence: Medford, ME (1860); Orneville, ME (1880); Bangor, ME (1900-1920)
individuals.interesting_facts:
John Lyman Frost was born in September 1855 in Maine, a son of Mark and Catherine (Gibberd) Frost. He is Kevin's great-great-grandfather and a central figure in the family's Penobscot County roots. John grew up in Medford, where the 1860 census records the Frost family, and came of age during the difficult post-Civil War years after his father's service and eventual death in 1877.

On June 2, 1878 (per FamilySearch; June 9, 1878, per the Piscataquis Observer newspaper), John married Carrie Wilder Storer in Bradford, Maine. At the time of his marriage he was living in Orneville, and the newspaper notice describes the wedding. The couple settled first in Orneville (1880 census) before eventually moving to Bangor, Maine's third-largest city, where they would spend the rest of their lives.

In Bangor, John and Carrie established themselves as part of the community. Census records from 1900 through 1920 place the family in Bangor, and by 1900, John's household included his widowed sister Clara R. Hubbard, showing the close bonds among the Frost siblings. John was the family anchor in the Bangor area, maintaining connections to the Lagrange community where his parents were buried.

John Lyman Frost died on November 29, 1921, in Bangor, Penobscot County, at the age of 65 or 66. He was laid to rest at Hill Crest Cemetery in Lagrange, joining his parents Mark and Catherine, his brother William, and his brother James in the family plot. His wife Carrie survived him by nearly three decades, living to the remarkable age of 93. Through their descendants, John and Carrie's line continues as the direct ancestral connection to the present-day family.

individuals.notes: Also called Calista, Clarista, or Etta. Graduated Maine Wesleyan Seminary 1881. Schoolteacher in Middlefield, CT. Married Albert R. Tucker 1883. One child: Albert Perry Tucker. FamilySearch PID: GS2H-B7Y.
individuals.source_citations: FindAGrave #70579066; Catherine Gibberd Frost FindAGrave bio; FamilySearch GS2H-B7Y
individuals.interesting_facts:
Etta Clarista J. Frost was born around 1858 in Maine, the sixth of seven children born to Mark and Catherine (Gibberd) Frost. Known by multiple names throughout her life — Calista, Clarista, and most commonly Etta — she was part of a generation of Frost children who would scatter across New England in search of opportunity.

Etta distinguished herself through education at a time when higher learning for women was still relatively uncommon. She attended and graduated from the Maine Wesleyan Seminary in 1881 — the institution now known as Kents Hill School, one of the oldest coeducational schools in America, founded in 1824. The seminary provided a rigorous education that prepared Etta for a career in teaching.

Following her graduation, Etta took a position as a schoolteacher in Middlefield, Connecticut, venturing far from her Maine roots. It was likely through her Connecticut connections that she met Albert R. Tucker, whom she married in 1883. The couple had one child, Albert Perry Tucker. Etta's journey from a farm girl in rural Piscataquis County to a seminary graduate and schoolteacher in Connecticut represents a remarkable trajectory of self-improvement in the late 19th century.

Etta died in 1920, though the exact date and location are not yet confirmed. Her FindAGrave memorial (#70579066) and FamilySearch record (PID: GS2H-B7Y) preserve her place in the family history. Among the Frost siblings, Etta's path was perhaps the most distinguished academically, and her story illustrates the opportunities that education opened for women in post-Civil War New England.

individuals.notes: Child of Mark and Catherine Frost. Full name Nellie Burton Frost. Married (1) William R Bateman, 5 Nov 1879 in Belmont, MA — 3 children (George William, May Elizabeth Gervin, Edward R). Married (2) Lincoln Coffin Allen, 16 Jun 1896 in Cambridge, MA. Adopted by Benjamin A Longley & Lydia A Hodgdon. Resided: 1860 Medford, ME; 1870 Cambridge, MA; 1900 Cambridge, MA; 1920 Medford, MA. FamilySearch PID: L7X7-G12.
individuals.source_citations: FamilySearch L7X7-G12 (45 sources attached)
individuals.residence: Medford, ME (1860); Cambridge, MA (1870-1900); Medford, MA (1920)
individuals.interesting_facts:
Nellie Burton Frost was born in September 1859 in Medford, Piscataquis County, Maine, the youngest child of Mark and Catherine (Gibberd) Frost. Her early life was marked by upheaval — her father Mark served in the Civil War and died when Nellie was about seventeen, and her mother Catherine eventually became a patient at the Maine Insane Hospital in Augusta.

Like her older sister Sarah, young Nellie was sent away from Maine to live with relatives or guardians. She was adopted by Benjamin A. Longley and Lydia A. Hodgdon, and by the 1870 census she was living in Cambridge, Massachusetts, at just ten or eleven years old. This early separation from her birth family was likely a consequence of the difficult circumstances at home, but it gave Nellie a new start in the thriving Boston metropolitan area.

Nellie married twice, building a family in Massachusetts. Her first marriage was to William R. Bateman on November 5, 1879, in Belmont, Massachusetts. Together they had three children: George William, May Elizabeth Gervin, and Edward R. After this marriage ended, Nellie married Lincoln Coffin Allen on June 16, 1896, in Cambridge, Massachusetts.

Nellie spent her adult life in the Greater Boston area — the 1900 census places her in Cambridge, and by 1920 she was living in Medford, Massachusetts (coincidentally sharing its name with the Maine town where she was born). She lived to nearly 80 years old, dying on April 6, 1939, in Malden, Middlesex County, Massachusetts. With 45 sources attached to her FamilySearch record (PID: L7X7-G12), Nellie is one of the better-documented Frost siblings, her Massachusetts life leaving a fuller paper trail than those who remained in rural Maine.

individuals.burial_place: Hill Crest Cemetery, Lagrange, ME
individuals.notes: Kevin's great-great-grandmother. Married John Lyman Frost June 2, 1878 in Bradford, ME (FamilySearch) or June 9, 1878 in Milo, ME (newspaper). Died in Bangor at age 93.
individuals.source_citations: FindAGrave #211431569; FamilySearch K2X9-RJL; Piscataquis Observer 13 Jun 1878
individuals.interesting_facts:
Carrie Wilder Storer was born on August 11, 1857, in Bradford, Maine, and would become Kevin's great-great-grandmother. She grew up in the small Penobscot County community of Bradford, and her middle name "Wilder" likely reflects a family connection on her mother's side.

On June 2, 1878, Carrie married John Lyman Frost in Bradford — an event also noted in the Piscataquis Observer newspaper of June 13, 1878, which recorded the date as June 9. The couple initially settled in Orneville before moving to Bangor, where they would spend the majority of their married life. In Bangor, Carrie and John raised their family and became established members of the community, appearing in census records from 1900 through 1920.

Carrie was widowed when John died on November 29, 1921, but she proved to be remarkably resilient. She continued living in Bangor for nearly three more decades, witnessing the Roaring Twenties, the Great Depression, World War II, and the dawn of the atomic age. Her extraordinary longevity meant she outlived not only her husband by 29 years but also most of her contemporaries.

Carrie Wilder Storer Frost died on October 19, 1950, in Bangor, Penobscot County, Maine, at the remarkable age of 93. She was laid to rest at Hill Crest Cemetery in Lagrange, beside her husband John Lyman Frost and near several other members of the Frost family. Her life spanned from before the Civil War to the mid-20th century — nearly a full century of American transformation. Her FindAGrave memorial (#211431569) and FamilySearch record (PID: K2X9-RJL) document her long life and her central place in the family's history.

individuals.burial_place: Hill Crest Cemetery, Lagrange, ME
individuals.notes: Died as child.
individuals.source_citations: FindAGrave, Hill Crest Cemetery, Lagrange, ME; FamilySearch family group records for John Lyman Frost (LYMX-G3L family)
individuals.interesting_facts:
John Andrew Frost was born around 1879, the first child of John Lyman Frost and Carrie Wilder Storer. His parents had married in June 1878 in the Bradford-Milo area of Piscataquis County, Maine, and were living in the Orneville Township area at the time of his birth. Tragically, John Andrew died in 1882 at approximately three years of age. The cause of his death is not recorded in surviving documents, though childhood mortality was common in rural Maine during this era, with diseases such as diphtheria, scarlet fever, and whooping cough claiming many young lives.

He was buried at Hill Crest Cemetery in Lagrange, Penobscot County, Maine, where many members of the Frost family would also be laid to rest over the following decades. John Andrew was the eldest of seven children; his younger siblings Ida, Edith, Rychie, Lewis, Eva, and Elsie all survived to adulthood. His early death is a poignant reminder of the fragility of life in 19th-century rural New England.

individuals.burial_place: Hill Crest Cemetery, Lagrange, ME
individuals.notes: Child of John Lyman Frost and Carrie Wilder Storer. FamilySearch PID: LYMX-TXG (8 sources). Never married. Listed on FamilySearch with companion Eva Louise Grant (1880-1975, PID M5D8-WSM); no marriage event recorded. Residences: 1900 Bangor Ward 1; 1910 Bangor Ward 6; 1930 Cleveland, Cuyahoga, Ohio; 1940 Cleveland Ward 15; 1950 Bangor, Maine. Lived in Cleveland, Ohio for ~20 years before returning to Maine.
individuals.source_citations: FindAGrave, Hill Crest Cemetery, Lagrange, ME; FamilySearch LYMX-TXG (8 sources); Islesboro Historical Society family records (fam6513); US Census 1900, 1910, 1930, 1940, 1950
individuals.interesting_facts:
Ida Ethel Frost was born on February 8, 1881, in Maine, the second child of John Lyman Frost and Carrie Wilder Storer. She grew up in the Bangor area, appearing in the 1900 census in Bangor Ward 1 as a young woman of 19 living with her parents and several siblings. By 1910, she was still in Bangor, living in Ward 6. Ida never married, but she shared her life with a close companion, Eva Louise Grant (1880-1975), who is listed alongside her on FamilySearch records.

At some point in the 1920s, Ida and Eva relocated to Cleveland, Ohio, where they lived for approximately two decades. The 1930 census places Ida in Cleveland, Cuyahoga County, and by 1940 she was residing in Cleveland Ward 15. The reasons for their move to Ohio are not documented, but Cleveland was a booming industrial city in that era, attracting many New Englanders seeking employment opportunities.

Ida returned to Maine later in life. The 1950 census records her back in Bangor, and she lived out her remaining years in the Penobscot County area. She died on January 31, 1961, in Bangor at the age of 79. She was buried at Hill Crest Cemetery in Lagrange, Maine, alongside other members of the Frost family. Eva Louise Grant survived her by fourteen years, passing away in 1975.
individuals.residence: Bangor Ward 1, ME (1900); Bangor Ward 6, ME (1910); Cleveland, Cuyahoga, OH (1930); Cleveland Ward 15, OH (1940); Bangor, ME (1950)

individuals.notes: Child of John Lyman Frost and Carrie Wilder Storer. FamilySearch PID: LYMX-55H (11 sources). Middle initial 'I' per FamilySearch; earlier records said 'Izola'. individuals.interesting_facts:
Edith I. Frost was born on November 22, 1882, in Maine, the third child of John Lyman Frost and Carrie Wilder Storer. Her middle initial 'I' appears consistently in FamilySearch records, though earlier family documents recorded her middle name as 'Izola.' She grew up in the Bangor area, appearing in the 1900 census in Bangor Ward 1 with her parents and siblings.

On April 14, 1906, Edith married Frank Albert Wilder (1882-1944) in Maine. Frank was originally from Greene, Androscoggin County, the son of Lewis Horton Wilder and Abbie Jane Sprague. After their marriage, the couple settled in Milo, a small town in Piscataquis County, where they would remain throughout their married life. Census records from 1910 through 1940 consistently place them in Milo. The couple had no children.

Frank Albert Wilder died on January 27, 1944, and Edith survived him by nearly four years. She died on December 29, 1947, in Maine, at the age of 65. She was buried in Penobscot County. Edith's long residence in Milo connected her to the Piscataquis County community where her parents had also lived during their early married years in neighboring Orneville Township.

individuals.interesting_facts:
Rychie Elba Frost was born on June 9, 1885, in Lagrange, Penobscot County, Maine, the fourth child of John Lyman Frost and Carrie Wilder Storer. Her unusual first name, sometimes appearing as 'Richie' in records, was distinctive even by the standards of 19th-century New England naming conventions. She grew up in the Bangor area, first appearing in census records in 1900 in Bangor Ward 1 at age 14.

Rychie never married and had no children. She lived in various locations in the Penobscot County area throughout her long life. By 1910 she was in Bangor Ward 6, and the 1930 census places her in Brewer, just across the Penobscot River from Bangor. She returned to Bangor Ward 6 by 1940, and by 1950 she was living in Lagrange, the town of her birth. She filed a Social Security claim on July 10, 1950.

Rychie's most remarkable distinction was her extraordinary longevity. She lived to the age of 102, dying on April 12, 1988, in Howland, Penobscot County, Maine. Her lifespan bridged the presidency of Grover Cleveland to that of Ronald Reagan, encompassing both World Wars, the Great Depression, the Space Age, and the dawn of the computer era. She was buried at Hill Crest Cemetery in Lagrange alongside her parents and siblings.

individuals.interesting_facts:
Lewis Edmund Frost was born on March 4, 1890, in Orneville Township, Piscataquis County, Maine, the fifth child of John Lyman Frost and Carrie Wilder Storer. He grew up in the Bangor area, appearing in the 1900 census in Bangor Ward 1 with his parents and siblings. Lewis was the only son in the family to survive to adulthood, his elder brother John Andrew having died in childhood in 1882.

When the United States entered World War I, Lewis registered for the draft in Penobscot County, Maine, and enlisted on April 29, 1918, at Great Works, Maine. He served until his discharge on June 20, 1919. After the war, Lewis eventually moved to Massachusetts. On June 28, 1922, he married Vera Louise Smith in North Attleboro, Bristol County, Massachusetts. The couple had one child, an infant son born in 1924 who tragically died in infancy.

Lewis lived in the North Attleborough area of Massachusetts through at least the 1930s and 1940 census. When World War II came, he again registered for the draft in 1942 in North Attleboro, though at age 52 he was not called to active service. After his first wife Vera, Lewis married Angelina Cora Moyen (1917-1989), a woman 27 years his junior, in Maine sometime after 1944. They had three children together.

By 1950, Lewis had returned to Maine and was living in Lagrange, Penobscot County. He died on July 12, 1962, in Bangor at the age of 72 and was buried at Hill Crest Cemetery in Lagrange alongside his parents and siblings.

She grew up in the Bangor area and appears in the 1900 census in Bangor Ward 1 as a child of seven, and in the 1910 census in Bangor Ward 6 as a young woman of 17.

Eva never married and had no children. After 1910, her life becomes difficult to trace in available records. FamilySearch contains an erroneous 1852 residence entry for her, which is clearly a data error since Eva was not born until 1892. She died in 1977 and was buried in Penobscot County, Maine, likely at Hill Crest Cemetery in Lagrange where many of her family members rest.

individuals.interesting_facts:
Elsie E. Frost was born on June 2, 1894, in Bangor, Penobscot County, Maine, the youngest child of John Lyman Frost and Carrie Wilder Storer. She was Kevin Frank's great-grandmother, making her a key figure in the direct maternal line. Elsie grew up in Bangor, appearing in the 1900 census in Ward 1 as a child of six and in the 1910 census in Ward 6 as a teenager of 15.

On September 2, 1916, Elsie married James Albert Mosher in Bangor. James was the son of Charles Henry Percy Mosher and Mary A. Crowell, also of the Bangor area. The couple settled in Bangor Ward 6, where they would remain throughout their married life. They had three daughters: Eleanor F. Mosher, who died in infancy; Mary Elizabeth Mosher, who would become Kevin's maternal grandmother; and Barbara Louise, who later married into the DeWolfe family.

James Albert Mosher died on April 26, 1945, one day before his 54th birthday, leaving Elsie a widow at age 50. Elsie survived her husband by more than two decades, living on in the Bangor area. She died on September 4, 1966, in Penobscot County at the age of 72. She was buried at Mount Hope Cemetery in Bangor, the second oldest garden cemetery in the United States, alongside her husband James.

individuals.interesting_facts:
James Albert Mosher was born on April 27, 1891, in Bangor, Penobscot County, Maine, the son of Charles Henry Percy Mosher (1859-1945) and Mary A. Crowell (1860-1944). He had two older sisters, Madeline Crowell Mosher (1885-1950) and Edith Mosher. James grew up in the Bangor area and lived in Bangor Ward 6 throughout his entire life.

During World War I, James registered for the draft, though the details of any active service are not recorded in available sources. On September 2, 1916, he married Elsie E. Frost in Bangor. Elsie was the youngest daughter of John Lyman Frost and Carrie Wilder Storer. The couple raised their family in Bangor Ward 6 and had three daughters: Eleanor (who died in infancy), Mary Elizabeth, and Barbara Louise.

James Albert Mosher died on April 26, 1945, in Maine, just one day before his 54th birthday. The cause of his death is not recorded in available sources. He was buried at Mount Hope Cemetery in Bangor. Both of his parents also died in the mid-1940s: his mother Mary in January 1944 and his father Charles in May 1945, just weeks after James himself. James was Kevin Frank's great-grandfather through his daughter Mary Elizabeth, who married Clifton Eugene Whitney.

Died in Berlin, VT (adjacent to Montpelier). FamilySearch PID: GSVM-7J6. Note: There is a duplicate Clifton E Whitney record (GSCF-938, 1915-2010) on FamilySearch that appears to be erroneous.

individuals.interesting_facts:
Mary Elizabeth Mosher was born on February 4, 1919, in Brewer, Penobscot County, Maine, just across the Penobscot River from Bangor. She was the daughter of James Albert Mosher and Elsie E. Frost, and Kevin Frank's maternal grandmother. Mary grew up in Bangor, appearing in census records there in 1920, 1930, and 1940. She had two sisters: Eleanor, who died in infancy, and Barbara Louise.

On June 9, 1941, Mary married Clifton Eugene Whitney in Bangor. Clifton was from Lee, Penobscot County, and had grown up in nearby Winn. Their marriage came just months before the United States entered World War II; Clifton would enlist in the Army in November 1942. The couple had at least two children: Gail Frances Whitney and Spencer Hale Whitney.

After the war, Mary and Clifton relocated to Montpelier, Vermont, where they were established by the 1950 census. Montpelier, the state capital, offered a different pace of life from the industrial cities of Maine. The family put down roots there, and Mary lived in the Montpelier area for over three decades.

Clifton died on November 21, 1979, and Mary survived him by four years. She died on November 4, 1983, in Berlin, Vermont, the town adjacent to Montpelier, at the age of 64. She was buried at Green Mount Cemetery in Montpelier alongside Clifton.

individuals.interesting_facts:
Clifton Eugene Whitney was born on November 28, 1918, in Lee, Penobscot County, Maine, the son of William G. Whitney (1888-1972) and Maye Mildred Farrington (1896-1975). Kevin's family remembers him as 'Clifford,' but all official records consistently show his given name as 'Clifton.' He grew up in Winn, a small town in Penobscot County, where his family appears in the 1930 and 1940 censuses. In 1940, he was 21 years old and still living with his parents, along with his brother and three sisters.

On June 9, 1941, Clifton married Mary Elizabeth Mosher in Bangor, Maine. The following year, after the United States entered World War II, Clifton enlisted in the Army on November 25, 1942, at Bangor. He served through at least July 1944, when records place him in Boston. The full extent of his wartime service, including any overseas deployment, is not documented in currently available sources.

After the war, Clifton and Mary relocated to Montpelier, Vermont, where they were living by the 1950 census. Montpelier, Vermont's capital city, would be their home for the rest of their lives. They raised at least two children: Gail Frances Whitney and Spencer Hale Whitney. Clifton's death registration was filed on November 23, 1979, in Berlin, Vermont.

Clifton Eugene Whitney died on November 21, 1979, in Berlin, Washington County, Vermont, exactly one week before his 61st birthday. He was buried at Green Mount Cemetery in Montpelier, one of Vermont's most historic cemeteries, alongside his wife Mary who would join him there four years later in 1983.

individuals.notes: Kevin's paternal grandfather. Full name: Joseph Franklin Frank. Christened 12 Apr 1925, Bridgeport, Logan Township, Gloucester, NJ. Youngest of 10 children of William Biddle Frank and Mary E. Farnkopf. Married Mildred Elsie Lewis. Father died when Joseph was 4 years old. Occupation: Operator (per 1950 census). Race: White. Buried in Gloucester County, NJ (cemetery TBD). FamilySearch PID: GDT8-FWZ. NJ Death Index: State File 20100066448. 9 siblings: William T (1896-1974), Laura (1898-1898), Augustus (1899-1986), Harry D (1902-1992), Harry H (1903-1992), George Reed (1903-1988), Anna Elizabeth Croft (1905-1992), Warren DuBois (1909-1966), Albert Jacob (1912-2001). 1940 census: married with infant daughter Mildred E (age 0) in Logan Township; Mildred wife listed as age 21 (born ~1918-1919, consistent with actual birth July 1918). Residence in 1935 was same house (Logan Township). Marriage likely ~1938-1939. 1950 census: 4 children listed — Mildred E (daughter, 10), Thomas W (son), Joseph D (son, 8), Davis G (son, 5). RESOLVED: 'Davis G Frank, son, age 5' in 1950 census IS Lewis G Frank (born 1944-07-15, age 5 on May 18, 1950). 'Davis' was a transcription error of 'Lewis'; the 'G' middle initial matches. Confirmed by Kevin Frank. Known children (from 1940/1950 census): Mildred E (~1940, ID 134), Thomas W (~1941, ID 135), Joseph D (~1942, ID 136), Lewis (1944, ID 22), and possibly Davis G (~1945, identity unclear). 1920 census (age 4, 'Joseph F Frank') in William B Frank household, Gloucester, NJ (Source 212). Mother's 1956 obituary confirms 'Joseph, of Swedesboro' as one of seven surviving sons. 1950 census (ark:/61903/1:1:6F9P-64PY) shows household: Joseph L Frank (head, age 34, operator, chemical plant), wife Mildred E Frank (age 31), daughter Mildred E (age 10), son Joseph D (age 8), son Davis G (age 5), son Thomas W. 'Davis G Frank' (age 5) is likely Lewis G Frank — matching middle initial and birth year (~1944-45). CHRISTENING: 12 Apr 1925 at Bridgeport, Logan Township, NJ (same date as brothers Warren and Albert — batch christening). FamilySearch PID: GDT8-FWZ. 1940 CENSUS (Source 225): Head of household at Bridgeport, Logan Township, age 24, married, born NJ. Wife E Mildred Frank age 21. Daughter M Mildred Frank age 0. Residence in 1935: same house.

individuals.burial_place: Clarksboro, East Greenwich Township, Gloucester, NJ
individuals.notes: Mother of Roswell Eldridge Lewis. Born 31 Oct 1867 in Goodhue, Minnesota (per FamilySearch). Census records list birthplace variously as New York (1900) and South Dakota (1910). EXPLANATION: Booth family lived in Burnside Township, Goodhue County, MN until ~1870-1880, then migrated to Sioux Falls, Dakota Territory. May was born in MN (1867) but grew up in SD — likely accounts for South Dakota birthplace claims in later censuses. May is ABSENT from 1880 Sioux Falls household (would be age ~12-13); may have been elsewhere at time of census or missed in indexing. Married William H Lewis in 1887. Widowed after William's death ~1925. Lived in East Greenwich Twp, Gloucester, NJ from ~1915 until death. Buried in Clarksboro, NJ — same town where granddaughter Mildred was born. FamilySearch PID: GXTR-LQZ. 1870 Census (ark:/61903/1:1:MDHS-8LH): May Booth, age 2, female, Minnesota, in Burnside Township, Goodhue, MN. Household head Richard H Booth (age 43, NY) and wife Sarah Booth (age 41, NY). Siblings in 1870: Kate (20), Richard (13), Carrie (11), Fred (9), Ida (7), Alice (3), Charlotte (0), Franklin (7), Irving (4). HIGH confidence parents: Richard H Booth (ID 141) and Sarah C Booth (ID 142) — confirmed by location match, FamilySearch birth record, and cross-reference with 1870/1880/1900 censuses. Updated 2026-03-31 Session 4.
individuals.source_citations: FamilySearch GXTR-LQZ; 1870 US Census ark:/61903/1:1:MDHS-8LH (Burnside Twp, Goodhue MN — May Booth, age 2); 1880 US Census ark:/61903/1:1:MCVW-8HC (Sioux Falls, Dakota Territory — May absent); 1900 Census Huntington, Suffolk, NY; 1910 Census Kent, DE; FindAGrave #100007615 (son Roswell, lists mother as Mary L Booth Lewis 1867-1942)

individuals.notes: Beryl's mother. Full name Violet Dorothy Sacre. Born Q3 1908 (Jul-Aug-Sep), Woolwich registration district, London. Maiden name Sacre — a Norman-French surname. Family memory records her as 'originally French, emigrated to England' but GRO birth index shows registration in Woolwich, London. The French connection likely refers to the family's ancestral origins rather than Violet's birthplace. Married William Joshua M Richards Q1 1930 in Lambeth, London. Family lived in south London (Woolwich, Camberwell, Southwark) 1930s, then moved to Sturminster, Dorset ~1940-42 (possibly WWII evacuation). At least 8 children. Resided Sturminster area of Dorset by 1942. Death date 10 October 1982 per WikiTree genealogy database (source 103). Birth date of 15 June 1908 per WikiTree — note possible conflict with GRO Q3 (Jul-Sep) 1908 registration; registration may have been delayed. Full parents now identified: father Edwin William G Sacre (ID 119), mother Edith Mary Ellis née Ellis (ID 120).

individuals.notes: Beryl's father. Full name William Joshua M Richards (third given name initial M, full name unknown). Born 7 May 1902, registered Q2 1902 in Lambeth, London (Vol 1D, p 331). Married Violet Dorothy Sacre Q1 1930 in Lambeth. Died September 1988, Greenwich, London. Family lived in south London (Woolwich, Camberwell, Southwark) during the 1930s, then relocated to Sturminster, Dorset ~1940-42 (possibly WWII-related). At least 8 children born 1931-1950. Family may have returned to London area after the war (youngest child Janet born London 1950). Died in Greenwich, suggesting he lived in south London in later life. Henry Richards (d. 1954) and Gertrude Richards (d. 1978) buried in Sturminster Newton Cemetery — possibly William's parents or relatives who remained in Dorset. Parents identified: William Richards (ID 183, b. ~1865, Lambeth, carman) and Sarah Richards (ID 184, b. ~1865, Lambeth). Identification based on 1911 census showing a 'William Richards, Son, M, 9, born Lambeth' in household of William and Sarah Richards in Lambeth — matching the only William Richards born Q2 1902 in Lambeth (William Joshua M). 1901 census of same household shows older brother Henry Richards (age 11), who later settled in Sturminster Newton, Dorset (d. 1954, FindAGrave #192271800). Henry's presence in Dorset likely drew William's family to evacuate there from London during WWII (~1940-1942). 1939 Register (Findmypast): William Richards, birth year 1902, residing at 196 Quinn Square, Waterloo, Lambeth, borough of Southwark, London. This confirms his south London residence immediately before WWII, and is consistent with the family relocating to Sturminster, Dorset ~1940-42.

individuals.notes: Kevin's paternal great-great-grandfather. Born 22 Mar 1824 in Nordheim, Württemberg, Germany. Christened 25 Mar 1824 in Nordheim. Died 2 Nov 1889 in Logan Township, Gloucester, NJ. German immigrant. Married Mary Magdeline Bundes (ID 71). 7 children. Parents: Johannes Franck (ID 162, 1788–1853) and Christine Friederike Rudolph (ID 163, 1787–1857), married 25 Nov 1817. Jacob was 4th of 7 children. FamilySearch PID: KZ2R-8GZ. Also listed as 'Jacob Franks' and 'Jacob Frank' in American records. OCCUPATION: Farmer (confirmed by 1853 Germans to America Index and 1880 US Census). IMMIGRATION: Probable arrival 30 April 1853 via ship Persian departing Havre (Le Havre, France). Father Johannes died 18 Feb 1853 — Jacob may have emigrated shortly after. RESIDENCE HISTORY: 1860 census — Woolwich Township, Gloucester, NJ; 1880 census — Upper Penns Neck, Salem, NJ (adjacent to Logan Township area); death 1889 — Logan Township, Gloucester, NJ. Confirmed burial: Bridgeport Cemetery (Saint Pauls Cemetery), Bridgeport, Gloucester County, NJ (FindAGrave #118358287). HEADSTONE NOTE: Grave headstone records birth year as 1823; christening record (more authoritative) gives 22 Mar 1824. CAROLINE FRANK NOTE: 1860 census does NOT show Caroline in the household — she would have been ~4 if born 1856. Either died before June 1860 or birth year is incorrect. FamilySearch lists 0 sources for Caroline (G6GK-939). 1870 CENSUS: Not yet found in indexed records. May be unindexed or surname variant not surfacing.

individuals.immigration: Emigrated from Germany to New Jersey (date unknown; before first NJ child born ~1856-1857)
individuals.notes: Kevin's paternal great-great-grandmother. Married Jacob Friedrich Franck (ID 70). 7 children: Caroline (1856), Frederic (1857), Charles G (1858), Minnie (1860), Elizabeth (1861), Kate (1865), William B (1869). Also listed as 'Mary Magdeline Bundes Franks'. FamilySearch PID: G6GV-X2D. CONFIRMED: Born in Germany (not New Jersey) — 1860 US Census shows birthplace as Germany. BIRTH YEAR CONFLICT: FamilySearch profile records birth year 1837; however, the 1860 census records her age as 28, implying birth year ~1832. This 5-year discrepancy requires further investigation. The 1837 date on FamilySearch appears to be unsourced user contribution. BUNDES SURNAME: The surname 'Bundes' does not appear in any US genealogical records indexed on FamilySearch, suggesting the name may have been preserved through family oral tradition. The surname is concentrated in the Rømø / Schleswig-Holstein / northern Germany region in European records, suggesting Mary's origin may be northern Germany or the Danish-German border region — geographically different from Jacob's Württemberg origin. They likely met in New Jersey after both emigrated separately. RESIDENCE: 1860 census places family in Woolwich Township, Gloucester, NJ. Family moved to Logan Township (Bridgeport area) before Jacob's death in 1889. Mary died 2 March 1910 in Logan Township, Gloucester, NJ. Burial presumed at Bridgeport Cemetery (Saint Pauls Cemetery), but no FindAGrave memorial found. NOTE: Caroline (birth 1856) is absent from the 1860 census — either died before June 1860, or the 1856 birth year in the FamilySearch tree is incorrect.
individuals.source_citations: FamilySearch G6GV-X2D; 1860 US Census, Woolwich Township, Gloucester, NJ — FamilySearch ark:/61903/1:1:MFHY-V4Q (listed as Mary Frank, female, age 28, born Germany)

individuals.notes: Elizabeth Malanoski Bailey's father. Born 25 March 1866 in Żałe, Brzuze, radziejowski county, Kuyavian-Pomeranian Voivodeship, Poland. Christened 28 March 1866 in same village. Birth name: Marian Malanowski (Polish form). Immigrated to the United States in 1888. Settled in Elmira, Chemung County, New York. Residences: 1900 Elmira Ward 6, Chemung, NY; 1905 Elmira Ward 06, Chemung, NY. Died 1929 in Elmira, NY. First wife: Leokadia Rogowski (1866-1919), married 1884 in Poland, 12 children. Second wife: Johanna Nowakowski (1889-deceased, FamilySearch LTKQ-DJ1), married 6 Sep 1919, New York, 2 children. Parents: Konstanty Malanowski (1835-1902, FamilySearch GSMB-ML1) and Wiktoria Cetkowska (1840-1903, FamilySearch GSMB-3ZL), married 1860 in Żałe. One of 6 siblings. FamilySearch PID: LTKQ-9YB (13 sources). Parents confirmed from FamilySearch: father Konstanty Malanowski (GSMB-ML1, born 1835 Poland, died 1902 Poland, now ID 132 in tree) and mother Wiktoria Cetkowska (GSMB-3ZL, born 1840 Poland, died 1903 Poland, now ID 133 in tree). Death date refined to 29 April 1929 from NY State Death Index entry 25129. Michael remarried 6 Sep 1919 to Johanna Novakowski (FamilySearch LTKQ-DJ1) — only ~4 months after Leokadia's death. Had 2 more children with Johanna.
individuals.source_citations: FamilySearch LTKQ-9YB (13 sources including Polish parish records, US Census 1900/1905); NY State Death Index 1880-1956, entry 25129, 29 Apr 1929 (FamilySearch ark:/61903/1:1:QGK9-KCXN, source 139).
individuals.interesting_facts: Born in the village of Żałe, Brzuze — a small village in Kuyavian-Pomeranian Voivodeship, Poland. individuals.burial_place: Saint Peter and Paul Cemetery, Elmira, Chemung County, New York
individuals.notes: Elizabeth Malanoski Bailey's mother. Born December 1866 in Russian-controlled Poland (parents' birthplace also listed as Poland Russia per 1900 US Census; specific parish unknown). Married Michael Marian Malanoski in 1884 in Poland (date per FamilySearch sources; 1900 census lists 14 years married, suggesting ~1886 — discrepancy noted). Immigrated to the United States in 1888. Settled in Elmira, Chemung County, New York. Residences: 1900 Elmira Ward 6, Chemung, NY (as 'Eliza Malanosky', age 34, birth Dec 1866, Poland Russia); 1905 Elmira Ward 06, Chemung, NY. Died 19 May 1919 in Elmira, NY (NY State Death Index entry 29716). Buried at Saint Peter and Paul Cemetery, Elmira, Chemung County, NY (FindAGrave memorial #151995906, confirmed 2026-03-31). Her death preceded her husband's second marriage (6 Sep 1919) by approximately 108 days. 12 children with Michael Malanoski: Elizabeth (1886), Jazeski (1888, died in infancy), Malanoski (1890, died in infancy), John Joseph (1891-1967), Zygmunt Stanislaus (1895-1987), Bernard Joe (1896-1964), Felix (1899-deceased), Robert Leo Lady Slaw (1901-1966), Wadlich (1902-deceased), Thomas J. (1903-1970), Stella V. (1907-1981), Stanislaus (1907, died in infancy). FamilySearch PID: GQV3-TQ7 (12 sources). Funeral notice in Elmira Star-Gazette 22 May 1919: service at family home, 1010 North Main Street, Elmira, and at St. Casimir's Polish Catholic Church at 9 a.m. Rev. Joseph A. Balcerak officiated requiem high mass. Pallbearers: Paul Mecraes, Vincent Sobkowski, John Gosiezki, Peter Scarveski, Jacob Droleski, William Schrienski. FamilySearch profile (GQV3-TQ7) confirms no parents listed — Rogowski Polish origins remain unresearched. MARRIAGE DATE CONFLICT: FamilySearch sources give 1884 for Poland marriage; 1900 US Census '14 years married' implies ~1886. The Polish record (1884) is likely more reliable than the census estimate.

individuals.residence: Canterbury, Kent (born 1885); Charlton and Kidbrooke, London (1901); Woolwich, London (1962 death)
individuals.notes: Violet Dorothy Sacre's mother. Maiden name Ellis. Married Edwin William G Sacre Q1 1904 in Woolwich (GRO Vol. 1D, p. 1366). FamilySearch PID GDNK-3SK. Birth date: FamilySearch ancestors page gives 31 July 1885; however the GRO birth registration is in Q4 1885 (Oct-Nov-Dec), which conflicts with a July 31 birth (which should register in Q3). The birth date of 31 July may be from the 1911 census and could be approximate, or the registration was significantly delayed. Using 1885 as confirmed birth year; exact date to be verified against GRO certificate. Born in Canterbury, Kent. 1901 census: Found as 'Edith M Ellis', age 15, born Canterbury, Kent, living in Charlton and Kidbrooke (Woolwich registration district) — same area Edwin W G Sacre lived. Siblings in household: Richard W Ellis (13, Dover, Kent), Arthur E Ellis (11, Charlton), Samuel J Ellis (8, Charlton). This confirms she was in Woolwich area before her 1904 marriage to Edwin Sacre. PARENTS CONFIRMED (session 14): Father: Richard Ellis (ID 130), born ~1850 Scotland, Clerk at Royal Arsenal Woolwich. Mother: Annie Ellis (ID 131), born ~1851 Farnham, Surrey. Her maiden name 'Ellis' confirmed by four children's birth registrations showing mother's maiden name Ellis. Died 29 November 1962 in Woolwich, Greenwich, London at age ~77.

individuals.notes: Biological mother of Edwin William G Sacre (ID 119). FamilySearch PID LB44-YCB. Born 1860 in Erith, Kent. Following her death, Edwin William Sacre remarried Ann Alice (~1892). Harriet's parents Thomas Gurr and Harriet Ann Benge are documented on her FamilySearch profile but not yet added to this tree BIRTH DATE CORRECTION (session 16, 2026-03-30): The England Births and Christenings record (FamilySearch ark:/61903/1:1:J39G-3QY) gives christening date 2 October 1859, Erith, Kent — NOT 1860 as shown in the FamilySearch Family Tree profile (LB44-YCB). The christening record clearly states 'Event Date: 2 Oct 1859' and 'Event Place: Erith, Kent, England'. Birth year is therefore 1859. The 1860 date in the FamilySearch profile appears to be an error. Birth date updated from '1860' to '~1859-10' (christening 2 Oct 1859, birth likely a few weeks prior).

individuals.notes: Father of Edith Mary Ellis (ID 120) and her siblings. Husband of Annie Ellis (ID 131). Born ~1850 in Scotland (per 1891 census). Occupation: Clerk at the Royal Arsenal, Woolwich (1891 census). Head of household in Charlton Next Woolwich in 1891 (household 975230) and 1901 (household 994159). Children at time of 1891 census: Ernest (Arthur Ernest, age 1), Emma (age 10, born Singapore), Richard (older son, age 13, born Kent), Edith M (age 5, born Kent). Children confirmed in 1901 census: Edith M (15, Canterbury), Richard W (13, Dover), Arthur E (11, Charlton), Samuel J (8, Charlton). Richard's death occurred between 31 March 1901 (when he is listed as head/husband in the 1901 census) and April 1911 (when Annie is listed as widowed). The 1901 household search in session 13 failed because Richard was born in Scotland, outside the expected birth range, and his record was not well-indexed under location-based searches. His presence in the 1901 household is confirmed via Annie Ellis's 1901 census record (wife, married, household 994159). Richard worked at the Royal Arsenal — the major government armaments facility in Woolwich — which explains the family's long residence in the Charlton/Woolwich area. The family also appears to have spent time in Singapore (daughter Emma born there ~1881) and moved through Canterbury and Dover before settling in Charlton by ~1890.

individuals.residence: 1880 Hector, Schuyler County, NY (single, farmer); 1900 Burdett, Schuyler, NY; 1920 Waverly, Tioga, NY (widowed)
individuals.notes: Robert L Miller's father. Pauline's paternal great-great-grandfather. FamilySearch PID: GS5Y-5KH. Full name Franklin J Miller (from NUMIDENT, ark:/61903/1:1:6K4J-YS7W). Head of household in 1900 US Census, Burdett, Hector Township, ED 50, Schuyler County, NY (household 277). Wife: Anna Everetts (born March 1857 NY). Two sons listed in 1900 census: Robert (born May 1883) and Earnest (born ~1886). In 1920 US Census (ark:/61903/1:1:MVSP-S5M): living in Waverly, Barton, Tioga County, NY, listed as widowed. Father's birthplace: New York. Mother's birthplace: New York. Occupation: farmer (based on rural Hector Township location in 1900). Anna died between 1900 and 1920 (Frank listed as widowed in 1920 census). 1880 US Census (Source 206): Frank Miller, age 24, single, farmer, head of household, Hector, District 3, Schuyler County, NY. Father born New York, mother born Delaware. Living with Mary Miller, F, 54, widowed, born Delaware, relationship "Other" — possibly his mother. CONFLICT: NUMIDENT (Source 204) names mother as "Lucy"; 1920 census (Source 203) says mother born NY; 1880 census says mother born Delaware. The 1880 Mary Miller may or may not be his mother — she is listed as "Other" not "Mother" in the relationship field. If Mary is his mother, her name discrepancy with "Lucy" could indicate: (a) her full name was Mary Lucy or Lucy Mary, (b) NUMIDENT was inaccurate (Frank was ~80 when he applied), or (c) Mary was not his mother. 1860 Census (Source 235): John F Miller, age 5, born ~1855, New York, in household of Mary Miller (age 45, Connecticut), William Miller (age 36, NY), Emmeline Miller (19, NY), Harrison Miller (17, NY), Mary L Miller (8, NY), Catharine, Schuyler County, NY (ark:/61903/1:1:MC4D-H6J). "John F" likely = Franklin ("Frank" / "John Franklin"). Catharine township is adjacent to Hector in Schuyler County. CONFLICT: This 1860 household head is Mary (45, born CT), not Robert — NUMIDENT names father as Robert Miller. William Miller (36) in household could be an uncle, older brother, or other relation.

individuals.residence: 1881: St George The Martyr Southwark (with parents); 1891: St George The Martyr Southwark (with parents, age 22); 1901: Lambeth, London; 1911: Lambeth, London
individuals.notes: Father of William Joshua M Richards (ID 60). Identified through 1901 and 1911 census records. Age 36 in 1901 (b. ~1865), age 44 in 1911 (b. ~1867) — typical census age discrepancy. Born Lambeth, London. Occupation: carman (horse-drawn cart driver), industry listed as 'rubbish contractor' in 1911. FamilySearch tree PID: GNGZ-YT9. Family lived in Lambeth (Kennington/Vauxhall SE area) through at least 1911. Parents identified via 1881 census (Source 224): father William Richards (ID 191, tinman, b. ~1839 Southwark) and mother Mary Ann Richards (ID 192, b. ~1843 Lambeth). In 1881, William was age 15, a scholar, living at St George The Martyr Southwark with parents and 5 siblings: Mary (16), Eliza (13), Emma (8), James (4), Betsy (2). FamilySearch tree links: this William is GT3T-MSY, father LXQS-P1Y, mother LXQS-SM5. 1891 CENSUS (Source 229): Still living in parents' household at St George The Martyr Southwark, listed as 'William Richards, Son, M, 22 years, London, England.' No wife or children present — appears to have been unmarried in April 1891. This means he married Sarah Parsons AFTER the 1891 census, likely in Q2 1893 (Source 230). Age 22 in 1891 gives birth ~1869, vs age 15 in 1881 (born ~1866), age 36 in 1901 (born ~1865), age 44 in 1911 (born ~1867) — typical census age variation, suggesting actual birth ~1865-1869. MARRIAGE RECORD FOUND (Source 230): William Richards married Sarah Parsons in Lambeth, Q2 1893 (Apr-May-Jun), Vol 1D, p 709. Cross-referenced via Sarah Parsons entry at same Vol/Page/Quarter (ark:/61903/1:1:2DFN-9DZ). This reveals Sarah's maiden name as PARSONS.
individuals.source_citations: 1901 Census (Source 213, ark:/61903/1:1:X9H6-B2B); 1911 Census (Source 214, ark:/61903/1:1:XWP1-G55); FamilySearch tree PID GNGZ-YT9; 1881 Census (Source 224, ark:/61903/1:1:Q27Q-QPVS); 1891 Census (Source 229, ark:/61903/1:1:QHF8-GMM); Marriage Registration Index Q2 1893 Lambeth (Source 230, ark:/61903/1:1:2DFN-C1R)

individuals.residence: Lambeth, London (1891, 1901, 1911); born St. Saviour Southwark
individuals.notes: Mother of William Joshua M Richards (ID 60). Identified through 1901 and 1911 census records. Age 36 in 1901 (b. ~1865), age 46 in 1911 (b. ~1865). BIRTH REGISTRATION FOUND: Sarah J Parsons, Q4 1866, St. Saviour Southwark, Vol 1D, Page 13 (Source 236). This is the SAME registration district where the Richards tinman family (IDs 191, 192) lived in 1881, 1891, and ~1899 — strongly suggesting the Parsons and Richards families were neighbors in Southwark. MAIDEN NAME IDENTIFIED: Parsons. Married William Richards in Lambeth, Q2 1893 (Apr-May-Jun), Vol 1D, p 709 (Source 230). 1891 CENSUS: Sarah Parsons, age 25, WIDOWED, Washer, Head of Household, Lambeth, with son Henry Parsons age 1 (Source 237). The 'widowed' status is likely a social convention to avoid stigma of illegitimacy — no marriage record for Sarah to anyone before the 1893 marriage to William Richards has been found in the GRO Marriage Registration Index. Henry (born Q2 1890 as Henry Hector Parsons) predates the Q2 1893 marriage by ~3 years. FamilySearch tree PID 9WF8-QGF (separate from the Richards tree entry) shows Sarah with spouse 'Henry Parsons' (9WF8-QG8) — this is likely an error; no corroborating marriage record found. Birth year discrepancy: GRO says 1866, 1891 census says ~1866 (age 25), 1901 census says ~1865 (age 36), 1911 census says ~1865 (age 46). The GRO birth index is the most reliable; the 1901/1911 ages are ~1 year off.

individuals.burial_place: Sturminster Newton Cemetery, North Dorset, Dorset, England (FindAGrave #192271800)
individuals.notes: Older brother of William Joshua M Richards (ID 60). BIRTH NAME: Henry Hector Parsons. Birth registered Q2 1890 (Apr-May-Jun), Lambeth, Vol 1D, Page 390 (Source 238). Registered under mother's maiden name Parsons — born ~3 years BEFORE parents' Q2 1893 marriage. 1891 Census: 'Henry Parsons, Son, M, 1 year' in household of Sarah Parsons (widowed, age 25) in Lambeth (Source 237). After mother married William Richards in 1893, Henry took the Richards surname. Listed as 'Henry Richards, Son, M, 11' in 1901 census (Lambeth). Not present in 1911 census household (aged ~21, moved out). Married Gertrude (ID 186). Buried at Sturminster Newton Cemetery, Dorset. FamilySearch tree PID for birth record: 9WF8-QLG (as Henry Hector Parsons). Middle name 'Hector' from GRO birth index — not used in later life (all census records show just 'Henry'). Age discrepancy: 1901 census age 11 (born ~1890) matches GRO birth Q2 1890. FindAGrave headstone says aged 65 at death Dec 1954, implying birth ~1889 — off by ~6 months (born Apr-Jun 1890, died Dec 1954 = age 64, headstone says 65).
individuals.source_citations: 1901 Census (Source 213, ark:/61903/1:1:X9H6-B2B); FindAGrave memorial #192271800 (Source 215); 1891 Census Lambeth as Henry Parsons (Source 237, ark:/61903/1:1:QC6P-BT2); GRO Birth Registration Index Q2 1890 Lambeth (Source 238, ark:/61903/1:1:2XY1-XNG)
individuals.interesting_facts: His residence in Sturminster Newton, Dorset likely drew his brother William's family to evacuate from London to the same town during WWII, where Beryl Richards (Pauline's mother) was born in 1944.

individuals.residence: 1881: St George The Martyr Southwark; 1891: St George The Martyr Southwark (St Saviour Southwark registration district)
individuals.notes: Father of William Richards (ID 183, born ~1866 Lambeth). Identified through 1881 census at St George The Martyr Southwark. Age 42 in 1881 (born ~1839). Birthplace recorded as Southwark, Surrey in census; FamilySearch tree (LXQS-P1Y) gives birthplace as Lambeth, Surrey — the two districts are adjacent and the boundary was often blurred. Occupation: Tinman (a worker in tin/sheet metal). FamilySearch PID: LXQS-P1Y. 1891 CENSUS (Source 229): Still living at St George The Martyr Southwark with wife Mary (age 50), and children William (22), Eliza (24), Emma (19), James (14), Betsy (12). Eldest daughter Mary (age ~26) no longer in household. Occupation recorded as 'Iron & Tin Plate Worker' — same trade as 'Tinman' from 1881. Age 52 in 1891 (born ~1839), consistent with 1881 age 42 (born ~1839). Registration district: St Saviour Southwark. Piece/Folio: 344/161, RG12. Household ID 655715. PARENTS IDENTIFIED via 1851 Census (FindMyPast GBC/1851/0004652309): father James Richards (tinman, b. ~1813, Cripplegate, Middlesex) and mother Elizabeth Richards (b. ~1815, Rotherhithe, Surrey). In 1851, William (age 12) was a scholar living with parents and 7 siblings in Christchurch, St Saviour Southwark. 1871 CENSUS (FindMyPast GBC/1871/0003870524): William Richards, Head, age 30, Tin plate worker, born Christ Church Surrey, living in St Mary Newington with wife Mary (28, b. St George Martyr) and children Mary (7), William (4), Lizer A (3).
individuals.source_citations: 1881 Census (Source 224, ark:/61903/1:1:Q27Q-QPVS); FamilySearch tree LXQS-P1Y; 1891 Census (Source 229, ark:/61903/1:1:QHF8-GMM)
individuals.interesting_facts: Three generations of William Richards: this William (tinman, b. ~1839), his son William (carman, b. ~1866, ID 183), and his grandson William Joshua M (b. 1902, ID 60).

individuals.notes: Kevin's paternal great-great-great-great-grandmother (gen 6). Mother of Christine Friederike Rudolph (ID 163). FamilySearch PID: LZ8R-XKG. Married Johann Stephan Rudolph 20 April 1771 in Nordheim. The von Olnhausen family were originally knights (Heinz von Otting zu Olnhausen), later settled in Nordheim by 1661 when Johannes von Olnhausen (1640–1698) became town judge and blacksmith. Johann Conrad von Ohlhausen (1674–1757) served as judge and mayor of Nordheim.

Father of William Richards (ID 191, tinman). Identified via 1851 Census (FindMyPast GBC/1851/0004652309). Occupation: Tinman. Born ~1813 in Cripplegate, Middlesex (City of London). In 1851, lived in Christchurch parish, St Saviour Southwark, with wife Elizabeth and 8 children: James (17, tinman), William (12, our ancestor), Maria (11), Christian (8), George (7), Thomas (6), Edward (5), Alice (infant). The tinsmith trade passed from father to son — both James Sr. and James Jr. listed as tinmen. 1841 census: living at Charlotte St, Great Broadwall, Christ Church, Southwark with wife Elizabeth and children James, William, Maria, Christian, and Elizabeth Pissell (b.~1799, possible mother-in-law). DEATH CONFIRMED: GRO Death Index Jun 1880, St Saviour Southwark, Vol 1d, Page 25, age 68 (born ~1812). Two possible Non-Conformist birth records in Cripplegate: (1) 31 Dec 1813, parents James Richards + Barbara Richards (ark:/61903/1:1:FWPS-GL2); (2) 29 Jun 1813, parents Robert Richards + Elizabeth Richards. Neither confirmed — need to order GRO death certificate to identify parents.
individuals.interesting_facts: Tinman by trade, same occupation as his son William (ID 191). Lived in Christchurch Southwark.

individuals.immigration: Arrived New England aboard the Pied Cow on 8 Jul 1634 with James Wall and John Goddard, under contract with Capt. John Mason of London's Laconia Company to build mills in Berwick.
individuals.notes: FS PID LHVM-B87. Baptized at Church of St Editha, Tamworth. Built first water-powered saw mill and grist mill in New England at Asbenbedick Falls (Great Works River) on 22 Jul 1634. First signer of the Kittery Act of Submission, 16 Nov 1652. Piscataqua Pioneer.
individuals.source_citations: FamilySearch Family Tree LHVM-B87; WikiTree Chadbourne-4; FindAGrave #46651181; Piscataqua Pioneers biography
individuals.interesting_facts: Built the first water-powered saw mill and grist mill in New England (1634). First signer of the Kittery Act of Submission.

individuals.notes: Married William Chadbourne 8 Oct 1609 at Tamworth. Father may be Thomas Sperry (per one source). It is not known when or where she died; she may have come to Maine but no account found in the New World.
individuals.source_citations: Tamworth Parish Register (marriage); WikiTree Chadbourne-4; FindAGrave #46651495
research_leads: Confirm father Thomas Sperry
Search Tamworth parish records for burial record

individuals.burial_place: Church of St Editha, Tamworth
individuals.notes: WikiTree Chadbourne-5. Married Margaret Dooley 28 Jan 1576 at Tamworth. Had at least 6 children baptized at St Editha: Robert, Margery, William, John, Randall, Thomas. Buried 16 Dec 1622 at Tamworth.
individuals.source_citations: Tamworth Parish Register (St Editha); WikiTree Chadbourne-5; FindAGrave #194561465

individuals.burial_place: Church of St Editha, Tamworth

individuals.immigration: Arrived in Boston 1634, possibly on the ship Griffin. Worked as blacksmith for James Everill.
individuals.notes: WikiTree Maxson-28. FindAGrave #102252438. One of the founders of Portsmouth (Pocasset), Rhode Island. Killed with his son Richard Jr. at Pelham Bay when they went ashore for provisions during an Indian uprising. English origins unknown per Robert Charles Anderson.
individuals.source_citations: WikiTree Maxson-28; FindAGrave #102252438; Maxson Family Association; Rhode Island Colonial Records
individuals.interesting_facts: One of the founders of Portsmouth (Pocasset), Rhode Island. Killed in 1643 Indian massacre at Pelham Bay along with his son.
research_leads: English origins unknown — search London blacksmith guild records for Maxson/Maggeson ~1620s

individuals.immigration: Arrived in Boston 1634 with husband Richard Maxson.
individuals.notes: Married Richard Maxson 2 Jan 1627/28 in London. After Richard's death in 1643, married John Harndell. Some sources claim she was Rebecca Marbury, daughter of Rev. Francis Marbury (father of Anne Hutchinson), but this connection is DISPUTED. Chamberlain and Clarenbach (1990) corrected earlier errors.
individuals.source_citations: WikiTree Maxson-28; Chamberlain and Clarenbach (1990); John Harndell will 1684/5
individuals.interesting_facts: Escaped the 1643 Pelham Bay massacre with children John and Rebecca in a small open boat back to Rhode Island.
research_leads: Verify maiden name — Marbury connection disputed
Search London marriage records for Maxson 1627/28

individuals.military_service: Served in Capt. Johnson's Roxbury company, marched from Boston 6 Jul 1675 for Mt. Hope during King Philip's War.
individuals.notes: Will dated 23 Oct 1693, proved 4 Mar 1694/5. Children include: Margaret (1656), Thomas, Mehitable, John (1662), William, Mary, Jane, Joseph, Hannah (1689), Benjamin, Ebenezer. 13 Apr 1684, children Thomas, William and John 'owned the covenant' in the Roxbury church.
individuals.source_citations: Cheney Genealogy by Charles Henry Pope (1897); Geni Thomas-Cheney-Sr
individuals.interesting_facts: King Philip's War veteran (1675).

individuals.notes: Married Thomas Cheney 11 Jan 1655 at Roxbury. Parents unknown — may be sister of Theodore Atkinson of Boston or daughter of Thomas Atkinson of Concord, but no evidence found.
individuals.source_citations: Cheney Genealogy by Charles Henry Pope (1897)
research_leads: Identify parents — check Theodore Atkinson of Boston or Thomas Atkinson of Concord

individuals.immigration: Probably came with the Great Fleet in 1630. In Boston 1635, then Newbury, then Roxbury by 1635.
individuals.notes: FS PID LZVG-D69. WikiTree Cheney-32. Immigrant ancestor. Founder of the Roxbury Free School (1664). Assessor 1648, constable 1654-56, selectman 1656-57. Possibly from Meynell Langley, Derbyshire. English parents UNKNOWN — previously claimed parents detached as unproven. Ancestor of President Taft and VP Dick Cheney.
individuals.source_citations: FamilySearch LZVG-D69; WikiTree Cheney-32; FindAGrave #52482669; Pope (1897); Anderson Great Migration
individuals.interesting_facts: Founder of the Roxbury Free School (now Roxbury Latin School). Ancestor of President William Howard Taft.

individuals.notes: Maiden name disputed: Cule, Cole, or Mason. One source says daughter of William Cole and Elizabeth Deards (unverified). Married William Cheney ~1624. After William's death 1667, married a Mr. Burge. Widow again by 1679. Moved to Boston.
individuals.source_citations: Cheney Genealogy by Pope (1897); WikiTree Unknown-438014
research_leads: Verify maiden name — Cule, Cole, or Mason? Parents unconfirmed

individuals.immigration: Before 1637, to Salem, Massachusetts; moved ~1641 to Wells, Maine
individuals.notes: Recorded in Salem 1638, may have been at Plymouth, then settled at Wells. Grand jury 1656. Parents disputed — one theory suggests Simon Wakefield and Elizabeth De Vere but this is unproven.
research_leads: Parents unknown — competing theories exist but none proven. English parish records from Yorkshire may help.

individuals.immigration: Immigrated with family to New England
individuals.notes: Baptised 22 Jul 1627. Daughter of Edmund Littlefield and Annis (maiden name unknown). FamilySearch, Geni, WikiTree all confirm.
individuals.interesting_facts: Daughter of Edmund Littlefield, early settler of Wells, Maine
research_leads: Search for Edmund Littlefield (father) records in Wells, Maine for further ancestry.

individuals.notes: Son of Daniel Goodwin (~1620, England - 1712/13, Maine) and Margaret Spencer (~1633-1670). Brother of Margaret Goodwin (wife of James Frost Sr.) is noted in existing records. His sister-in-law Mehetable Goodwin was captured by Indians.
research_leads: Daniel Goodwin (father, ~1620, England) and Margaret Spencer (mother, ~1633-1670) are documented — could extend line further.

individuals.notes: WikiTree (Taylor-7826). Daughter of John Taylor. Married William Goodwin Oct 1687 in Berwick.
research_leads: Father identified as John Taylor — search for more details on John Taylor in Berwick area.

individuals.burial_place: Glemsford, Suffolk, England
individuals.notes: Clothier of Glemsford, Suffolk. Will dated Feb 1565, proved 27 May 1565. Named children: John, Edward, Ann (wife of John Frost), Katherine, Mary, Margaret, Joanna. Married second wife Johan Tollington (widow of Christopher) on 8 Oct 1556. Some sources suggest parents John Scott and Ann Pympe but this requires verification.
individuals.interesting_facts: Ancestor of Richard Scott who emigrated to Providence, Rhode Island
research_leads: Parents possibly John Scott and Ann Pympe — needs verification from parish records.

individuals.burial_place: Glemsford, Suffolk, England
individuals.notes: First wife of Richard Scott, clothier of Glemsford. Buried 23 August 1556 at Glemsford. Mother of Ann Scott (wife of John Frost). Maiden name Pigot per WikiTree (Scott-1649) and Geni profiles.
research_leads: Pigot family of Suffolk — search Glemsford parish registers for her baptism record.

individuals.notes: Also spelled Horsum. Father of Hannah Hersom (wife of Mark Frost), Joshua Hersom, Benjamin Hersom, Molly Hersom, David Hersom, Elizabeth Hersom, Olive Hersom.
research_leads: Search Berwick vital records for birth/death dates. Search for parents in Hersom family of South Berwick.

individuals.notes: Born 28 Feb 1728, Berwick, York, Maine. Maiden name Nock. Descendant of Thomas Nock (1617-1666) line. Wife of John Hersom. Mother of Hannah Hersom (wife of Mark Frost).
research_leads: Search for Nock family in Berwick/South Berwick area for her parents.

individuals.notes: Father of Sarah Ford (wife of Nathaniel Whitney), John Ford Jr. (1680-1713), Lettice Ford, Catherine Ford, Samuel Ford. Early settler of Kittery, Maine.
research_leads: Search Kittery vital records and York County deeds for more details.

individuals.notes: Maiden name Andrews. Daughter of John Andrews of Braveboat Harbor, Kittery, Maine (~1600-1671) and Joane Andrews. Sister of Sarah (Andrews) Mitchell and Elizabeth (Andrews) Winchester.
research_leads: Father John Andrews (~1600-1671) of Braveboat Harbor, Kittery is documented — could extend maternal line through him.

individuals.notes: Of Langham, Co. Suffolk. Named as father of Prudence (wife of John Frost) in 'The Frost Family in England and America' published genealogy. No parish register or probate record located for Henry himself.
research_leads: Search Suffolk parish registers for Henry Valyant baptism/burial in Langham area. Valyant/Valiant is an unusual surname.

sources.description: United States, Germans to America Index, 1850-1897 — Jacob Frank, age 28, Württemberg, ship Persian, Havre, 30 Apr 1853
sources.location: New York, New York, United States
sources.notes: Probable match for Jacob Friedrich Franck (ID 70). Jacob Frank, male, age 28 (estimated birth 1825 vs actual 1824 — 1-year discrepancy typical of passenger records), birthplace Württemberg, occupation Farmer. Departure port Havre (Le Havre, France). Ship: Persian. Arrived 30 April 1853. No image available — index only. Confirmed as probable match based on: Württemberg origin, age ~29, occupation Farmer (matching 1880 census), pre-1856 arrival consistent with first child born NJ ~1857.
