sources.notes: Children on memorial: William T (1896-1974), Augustus Farenkoph (1899-1986), Harry Henry (1902-1992), George Reed Sr (1903-1988), Warren DuBois (1908-1966), Albert Jacob (1912-2001). Joseph Franklin and Anna/Hannah not listed.

sources.description: Find a Grave — Mary Elizabeth Farnkopf Frank, memorial #118358084, Bridgeport Cemetery, Bridgeport, Gloucester County, NJ. Birth 6 Nov 1877, Salem, Salem County, NJ. Death 7 Mar 1956 (aged 78), Philadelphia, Philadelphia County, PA. Headstone photo: 'MARY E. FRANK 1877 — 1956'. Obituary clipping attached: 'Mrs. Mary E. Frank Dies in Philadelphia' — died Wednesday in St. Joseph's Hospital, Philadelphia; resident of 85 Main Street, Bridgeport for 60 years. Survived by daughter Mrs. Hannah E. Craft of Northport, NY, and seven sons: William T., August, Harry and Albert of Bridgeport; Joseph of Swedesboro; George of Repauno; Warren of Pedricktown. Services at Carlton Coles Funeral Home, Swedesboro. Interment at Bridgeport Cemetery. Memorial added by Gary Chaffin; obituary clipping added by Tom Jennings.
sources.location: Bridgeport Cemetery, Bridgeport, Gloucester County, NJ
sources.notes: Obituary published ~March 9, 1956 (Bridgeport, N.J., March 9 — Special). Confirms 8 surviving children in 1956. Mother listed as Mary Ellen Haverly Farenkopf (1848-1920). Parents confirm Augustus Farenkopf (1847-1916) and Mary Ellen Haverly.

sources.notes: Augustus (age ~21) not in household — likely moved out. Laura (d. 1898 infant) deceased. Anna S Frank (age 14) is likely Hannah E. Craft from Mary's obituary.

sources.description: England and Wales Census 1891 — William Richards, Head, age 52, Iron & Tin Plate Worker, born London. Wife: Mary, age 50, born London. Children: Eliza (24), William (22), Emma (19), James (14), Betsy (12). All born London. St George The Martyr Southwark, Southwark, London. Registration District: St Saviour Southwark. Piece/Folio: 344/161, RG12. Household ID 655715.
sources.location: St George The Martyr Southwark, London, England
sources.notes: Confirms tinman William Richards (ID 191) and family still in same Southwark location as 1881. Son William (ID 183) age 22 still at home with no wife — he was not yet married to Sarah. FamilySearch tree match: LXQS-P1Y.

sources.description: England and Wales Marriage Registration Index — William Richards married Sarah Parsons, Q2 1893 (Apr-May-Jun), Lambeth, London. Vol 1D, p 709.
sources.notes: Cross-referenced: both William Richards and Sarah Parsons entries share identical Vol 1D, p 709, Q2 1893, Lambeth — confirming they married each other. Possible co-registrants also listed (Alice Davies, William Sturgeon) but the Richards-Parsons pairing is confirmed by the matching page numbers. Reveals Sarah's maiden name as PARSONS. From 'England & Wales Marriages, 1837-2005' via findmypast, sourced from General Register Office, Southport.

sources.description: England and Wales Death Registration Index — William Richards, age 59, 1899, St. Saviour Southwark, London. Vol 1D, p 64.
sources.notes: TENTATIVE match for William Richards the tinman (ID 191). Age 59 gives birth ~1840, close to ~1839 from census records. Same registration district (St Saviour Southwark) as 1891 census. However, William Richards is a common name — needs verification via GRO death certificate order. Not yet linked to tree individual.

sources.description: United States Census 1880 — Anna M. Everts, age 23, single, occupation 'At Home', daughter, in household of Aranthus W. Everts (51, NY) and Harriet Everts (47, NY), Montour, ED 149, Schuyler County, NY. Siblings in household: James L (24), Hannah (19), Alpha E (14), Eva C (14), Aranthus W (11), Clara H (7). Father's birthplace: NY. Mother's birthplace: NY. Sheet A, #388.
sources.notes: CRITICAL: Anna M. Everts listed as SINGLE in 1880, still living with parents. She married Franklin Miller ~1881 after this census. Attached to FamilySearch tree person GN1Q-71S.

sources.description: United States Census 1860 — John F Miller, age 5, born ~1855, New York, in household of Mary Miller (45, Connecticut) as head, William Miller (36, NY), Emmeline Miller (19, NY), Harrison Miller (17, NY), Mary L Miller (8, NY). Catharine, Schuyler County, NY. Page 43, NARA M653.
sources.notes: John F Miller (age 5, born ~1855) is likely our Franklin J Miller (ID 171). 'John F' could be John Franklin or Franklin with middle initial J. Catharine township is adjacent to Hector in Schuyler County. No 'Robert' or 'Lucy' in this household — conflicts with NUMIDENT parent names.

sources.description: WikiTree profile for John Wakefield (Wakefield-92); Geni.com profile; Noyes, Sybil I., 'Genealogical Dictionary of Maine and New Hampshire'
sources.notes: Multiple sources confirm John Wakefield of Wells, Maine married Elizabeth Littlefield.

sources.description: WikiTree profiles for William Goodwin (Goodwin-957) and Deliverance Taylor (Taylor-7826); Geni.com profiles; multiple compiled genealogies
sources.notes: William Goodwin son of Daniel Goodwin and Margaret Spencer. Deliverance Taylor daughter of John Taylor.

sources.description: Will of Richard Scott, clothier of Glemsford, dated Feb 1565, proved 27 May 1565. WikiTree (Scott-1649); Geni profiles. Will names son-in-law John Frost, left him two leases of 'My Polye' of Boxted, looms at Howldens, and 40 shillings.
sources.notes: Richard Scott's will confirms Ann as his daughter and John Frost as son-in-law.

sources.description: WikiTree HERSOM genealogy page; Geni.com profiles for Joshua Hersom listing parents John Hersom and Olive (Nock) Hersom; multiple family trees agree
sources.notes: Multiple genealogy sites list John Hersom and Olive Nock as parents of Hannah Hersom and siblings Joshua, Benjamin, Molly, David, Elizabeth, Olive.

sources.description: Geni.com profiles for John Ford Jr. (son) listing parents John Ford and Joanna (Andrews) Ford; WikiTree Whitney-377 (Abel Whitney profile listing Sarah Ford as mother); Early Settlers of Kittery project on Geni
sources.notes: Multiple Geni profiles list Sarah Ford as daughter of John Ford and Joanna Andrews of Kittery, Maine.

sources.description: The Frost Family in England and America (published genealogy, Library of Congress). Pedigree chart names Prudence as daughter of Henry Valyant of Langham, Co. Suffolk. Also WikiTree (Frost-3600). Prudence's will dated 10 Jun 1636, proved 9 Aug 1641 by son James in Archdeaconry Court of Sudbury.
sources.notes: Published Frost family genealogy held by Library of Congress identifies Henry Valyant as Prudence's father.
